fileChanges.diff:
diff --git a/CLAUDE.md b/CLAUDE.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..1ec5eb7
--- /dev/null
+++ b/CLAUDE.md
@@ -0,0 +1,34 @@
+# Project Guidelines
+
+## Architecture
+- This project follows the repository pattern for data access
+- Services depend on repositories, never on each other directly
+- Events are preferred over direct cross-service calls for decoupling
+- All business logic lives in the service layer
+
+## Coding Conventions
+- Use TypeScript strict mode — no `any` types
+- All public methods must have JSDoc comments
+- Use dependency injection via constructor parameters
+- Error handling: throw typed errors, never return null for errors
+- Prefer async/await over raw Promises
+
+## Testing
+- Tests use vitest
+- Each module has a corresponding test file in tests/
+- Mock external dependencies, test business logic directly
+- Minimum: test the happy path and one error path per public method
+
+## File Organization
+- src/models/ — Data models and interfaces
+- src/repositories/ — Data access layer (implements repository interfaces)
+- src/services/ — Business logic layer
+- src/events/ — Event definitions and event bus
+- src/utils/ — Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
+- src/config/ — Configuration files
+- tests/ — Test files mirroring src/ structure
+
+## Git Practices
+- Commit atomically per logical change
+- Write descriptive commit messages explaining the "why"
+- Run tests before committing

diff --git a/src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts b/src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..4fb193b
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,23 @@
+/**
+ * NotificationAdapter interface — the contract all notification channels must implement.
+ *
+ * Architecture constraint: Every notification channel (email, webhook, SMS, in-app)
+ * must implement this interface. The NotificationService routes payloads to adapters;
+ * adapters handle channel-specific rendering and delivery.
+ *
+ * Adapters MUST NOT subscribe to EventBus directly. Only NotificationService
+ * subscribes to events and delegates to adapters.
+ */
+
+import type { NotificationChannel, NotificationPayload, NotificationResult } from '../models/notification.js';
+
+export interface NotificationAdapter {
+  /** The channel this adapter handles. */
+  readonly channel: NotificationChannel;
+
+  /**
+   * Send a notification through this channel.
+   * Must return a result indicating success or failure — must not throw.
+   */
+  send(payload: NotificationPayload): Promise<NotificationResult>;
+}

prompt:
You are Agent 1 of 12 working on a codebase at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-y72alZ.

## Codebase Orientation
This is a TypeScript service (~1,140 lines, ~24 files).

Directory structure:
```
src/
  models/        # Data models and interfaces
  services/      # Business logic layer
  repositories/  # Data access layer
  utils/         # Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
  events/        # Event definitions (if using event-driven pattern)
  adapters/      # External service adapters (email, webhook, etc.)
  validators/    # Validation logic
  config/        # Configuration
tests/           # Test files mirroring src/ structure
```

Your task: Design and implement the foundation of a notification system.

Specifically:
1. Choose an architecture for notifications: event-driven (EventBus with subscribers) OR direct service calls. Commit to ONE approach.
2. Define the notification service interface and core types (NotificationType, NotificationPayload, etc.) in src/services/ and src/models/.
3. Implement the base notification service with the chosen architecture.
4. Create an email notification adapter in src/adapters/email.adapter.ts (stub is fine, but interface must be real).
5. Document your architectural decision — the pattern you chose, why, and what constraints it imposes.
6. Make sure the codebase compiles.

Important:
- Pick ONE pattern (event-driven OR direct calls) and commit to it. Do not mix approaches.
- Define clear interfaces that other developers will implement against.
- Document constraints: e.g., "All adapters must implement NotificationAdapter interface" or "All notifications must go through EventBus.emit()".
- 11 more developers will build on your foundation.

parameters.task: Design and implement notification system foundation: architecture choice (event-driven vs direct calls), core types, notification service, email adapter

parameters.description: List all files in the repo

parameters.prompt:
Read ALL of these files completely and return their full contents:
- src/events/event-bus.ts
- src/events/event-types.ts
- src/services/notification.service.ts
- src/services/order.service.ts
- src/services/user.service.ts
- src/models/order.ts
- src/models/user.ts
- src/repositories/base.repository.ts
- src/repositories/user.repository.ts
- src/repositories/order.repository.ts
- src/utils/logger.ts
- src/utils/database.ts
- src/utils/pagination.ts
- src/utils/callback-registry.ts
- src/config/app.config.ts
- src/index.ts

Return each file's complete content with its path as a header.

cons: Every new channel requires touching existing services
alternatives.reason_rejected: Tight coupling between services; adding a new notification channel requires modifying every service that triggers notifications. Doesn't scale for 11+ developers working in parallel.

cons: Two systems to understand and maintain
alternatives.reason_rejected: Inconsistency creates confusion for developers. The existing code comments explicitly call this out as a problem to fix.

constraints: All notifications must go through EventBus.emit() — no direct service-to-service notification calls
All notification channels must implement the NotificationAdapter interface
NotificationService is the sole EventBus subscriber for notification concerns — individual adapters do not subscribe directly
parameters.context: The codebase has an existing EventBus but uses a mixed pattern: order creation goes through EventBus while status changes use CallbackRegistry. 11 more developers will build on this foundation. Need a single, consistent pattern with clear adapter interfaces.
parameters.rationale: Event-driven via EventBus is the right choice because: (1) EventBus already exists and is the primary pattern, (2) it provides loose coupling — new notification channels can be added without modifying existing services, (3) the event log enables debugging and replay, (4) async handlers support future needs like external API calls. All notifications flow through EventBus.emit() → NotificationService (subscriber) → NotificationAdapter implementations (email, webhook, etc.). The CallbackRegistry pattern for status changes will be replaced with EventBus.
parameters.summary: Unified event-driven architecture for all notifications via EventBus + NotificationAdapter interface

parameters.detail:
Implemented:
1. **Architecture decision**: Unified on event-driven (EventBus only). Removed CallbackRegistry usage from order status changes.
2. **Core types** in `src/models/notification.ts`: NotificationType, NotificationChannel, NotificationPayload, NotificationResult, Notification
3. **NotificationAdapter interface** in `src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts`: contract all channels must implement
4. **EmailNotificationAdapter** in `src/adapters/email.adapter.ts`: stub implementation with in-memory tracking
5. **Refactored NotificationService** in `src/services/notification.service.ts`: subscribes to all 4 event types, routes through adapters, records all notifications
6. **Fixed OrderService** in `src/services/order.service.ts`: status changes now use EventBus.emit() instead of CallbackRegistry

Constraints for other developers:
- All notifications MUST go through EventBus.emit() — no direct service-to-service calls
- All channels MUST implement NotificationAdapter interface
- Only NotificationService subscribes to EventBus for notification concerns — adapters must not subscribe directly
- New notification types require: (a) add NotificationType variant, (b) add event handler in NotificationService
parameters.summary: Agent 1: Notification system foundation complete — unified event-driven architecture via EventBus + NotificationAdapter

parameters.command:
git add src/models/notification.ts src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts src/adapters/email.adapter.ts src/services/notification.service.ts src/services/order.service.ts src/index.ts && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Unify notification system on event-driven architecture via EventBus

Replace mixed EventBus/CallbackRegistry pattern with pure EventBus for
all notification flows. Add NotificationAdapter interface, core notification
types (NotificationType, NotificationPayload, NotificationResult), and
EmailNotificationAdapter stub. NotificationService now subscribes to all
4 event types and routes through registered adapters.

Constraints for downstream developers:
- All notifications must go through EventBus.emit()
- All channels must implement NotificationAdapter
- Only NotificationService subscribes to events for notification concerns

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
parameters.description: Stage and commit notification system foundation
